texto: Hakeem: A wise man once said that suffering produces perseverance, character; and character, hope.Andre: Since when did spouting masochism make one wise? And the sacraments of a bitter existence? Who deemed that a vaunted prize? Nihilistic philosophy only births more pain. It's fruitless to espouse folly, repackage it as wisdom, and spew it in a wise man's name.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Booker T. Mattison que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Hakeem: A wise man once said that suffering produces perseverance, character; and character, hope…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, thought-provoking, character, com foco em reflexao.
